focus: Add a public root search interface and indexed-data details so users can try the live Cranfield baseline without reading source code or raw API docs.
reason_started: The operator noted that users need a search interface and details of indexed data so they know what to search for.
reason_ended: The interface, metadata endpoints, local tests, Cloudflare deployment, and two public validation passes after VALSTRAT-004 stabilized are complete.

validators_executed.name: npm run validate
validators_executed.evidence: 16 tests passed, including root UI and metadata endpoint contract tests.

validators_executed.name: public root UI checks
validators_executed.evidence: Root URL served a first-screen search interface with indexed-data details and example queries.

validators_executed.name: public metadata endpoint checks
validators_executed.evidence: Metadata endpoints returned Cranfield counts, fields, topic hints, and sample queries.

validators_executed.name: public search/explain checks
validators_executed.evidence: Live search and explain worked through latest aliases and canonical Cranfield endpoints.

validators_executed.name: second validation pass
validators_executed.evidence: After VALSTRAT-004 stabilized, public UI, metadata, search, and explain behavior passed twice with additional queries.

validators_skipped.name: browser visual screenshot
validators_skipped.reason: The browser plugin reported no available browser instances. Public HTTP and contract validation were still completed.

github_submission.blocker: Interface and evidence changes remain in the working tree for review; no explicit commit or push request was made.

reusable_lessons: Public search APIs need a root interface and dataset profile before they are genuinely tryable by non-technical users.
Dataset metadata should be available both in the UI and as JSON so articles, agents, and users can discover what searches are likely to work.
downstream_impacts: Phase 1 article can link to a usable search UI instead of only raw API examples.
Future BEIR and ESCI phases should add comparable dataset metadata before public endpoint release.
content_seeds: Phase 1 article can include screenshots or examples from the public search interface.
evidence_quality_notes: Functional evidence is strong: local tests and two public validation passes after the UI validation strategy stabilized.
Visual screenshot evidence is absent because the browser connector had no available browser instance.
Point latency evidence was collected during public checks; p95 still needs sampled measurement before public latency claims.

supports_mission: Makes the public endpoint directly tryable.
Teaches users what Cranfield contains and why certain queries work.
Keeps the baseline transparent by pairing search results with explain output.
missing: Browser screenshot validation when browser tooling is available.
Phase 1 article linked to commit and endpoint.
goal_drift_review.mission_alignment: GEN-005 improves Phase 1 public usability without adding unearned search architecture.
